name: Chicken-Cheese Crepes
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces Frozen peas (or 10 ounces fresh peas)
recipeInstructions:
(Note: Make crepes first; then they'll be ready when the sauce and filling are finished.)
Crepes: Mix all crepe ingredients in a bowl and beat with a rotary beater until blended.
Heat a lightly greased 6-inch skillet. Remove from heat. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of batter into the skillet, lifting and tilting the skillet to spread the batter evenly. Return to heat; brown on one side only. (Alternatively, cook on an inverted crepe pan.) Invert the pan over paper toweling; remove the crepe. Repeat to make 16-18 crepes, greasing the skillet occasionally.
Unfilled crepes freeze well. Store in your freezer by stacking them and alternating each crepe with 2 layers of waxed paper. Then, wrap the stack in a moisture-vaporproof bag. Freeze up to 4 months. Let crepes thaw at room temperature for about 1 hour before using.
Cheese sauce: In a medium saucepan, melt butter. Stir in flour and salt. Add milk, water, and chicken bouillon granules all at once. Cook over medium heat, stirring until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir for an additional 1-2 minutes. Stir in cheese, wine, parsley, and Tabasco. Remove 1 cup of the cheese sauce and set aside. Stir drained mushrooms into the remaining sauce.
Filling: Cook peas according to package directions; drain. Combine peas, chicken/turkey, pimiento, and the reserved 1 cup of cheese sauce. Spread about 1/4 cup of filling over the unbrowned side of each crepe, leaving a 1-inch rim around the edge. Roll up each crepe. Place seam-side down in a 12x7x2 inch baking dish. Repeat with remaining crepes. Pour the remaining cheese sauce over the crepes. Sprinkle with paprika if desired.
Cover; bake at 375°F for 18-20 minutes or until heated through. Let stand 10 minutes before serving.
